findings.description: The North Carolina Marine Fisheries logo/seal image present in the PDF header is missing from the HTML conversion.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows the NC Marine Fisheries seal/logo in the upper left corner. The HTML version has no image/logo present.

findings.description: The PDF contains a typo 'UNLAWFULLTO' (missing space), and the HTML corrects this to 'UNLAWFUL TO' with a space added. While a minor text change, it alters the exact text of the legal proclamation.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'IT SHALL BE UNLAWFULLTO USE POTS' | HTML: 'IT SHALL BE UNLAWFUL TO USE POTS'

findings.description: The document has no heading hierarchy beyond the single h1 for 'PROCLAMATION'. The major sections (Roman numerals I-X, county names) are rendered as bold text within list items rather than as proper subheadings. While this is a structural choice, the lack of any h2/h3 headings for significant sections like county groupings and GENERAL INFORMATION flattens the document structure.
findings.evidence: Sections like 'CARTERET COUNTY/ONSLOW COUNTY', 'ONSLOW COUNTY', 'PENDER COUNTY', 'NEW HANOVER COUNTY', 'BRUNSWICK COUNTY', and 'GENERAL INFORMATION' are all bold text within <li> elements rather than headings.

findings.description: The page footer text ('State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries | 3441 Arendell Street | P.O. Box 769 | Morehead City, North Carolina 28557 | 252-515-5500') that appears on each page of the PDF is omitted from the HTML, as are page headers like 'Proclamation PT-2-2024 Pg. 2'. This is acceptable removal of repeated boilerplate.
findings.evidence: Footer appears on all 3 pages of PDF but not in HTML. This is acceptable behavior per calibration guidelines.
